paragraphs.title: Contacting Me Outside of Class
paragraphs.p: The best way to contact me outside of class is with email. You should include a relevant subject in the Subject: header of your e-mail. The subject should include the class you are taking (including the course number and CRN) and the topic of your message. For example, you email subject line might be: [MTH 60 - CRN 40026]: Help with Section 1.1 Homework If you treat your email subject line this way, it helps me prioritize getting back to you quickly. I sometimes get a hundred or more emails in just one day, and I want your emails (my student's emails) to be the ones I prioritize.
You can also find me in Office Hours as described in .

paragraphs.title: Responses from Me
paragraphs.p: You can expect me to reply to your emails and discussion posts within 48 hours, and often much sooner. If you send me a message in the evening, it is more likely that I will not receive your message and reply until the following day. This means that if you wait until the evening an assignment is due to reach out to me for help, then I might not get to replying until after the assignment is due.
